Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with three cases We need to produce a process tree that captures the behavior Lets analyze the traces Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC Observations All cases start with Receive Application RA then Validate Form VF then Assess Eligibility AE So a sequence RA VF AE

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After AE there may be a loop Request Missing Info RMI and then Validate Form VF again In case 1 after AE RMI then VF then AD In case 2 after AE directly AD no RMI In case 3 after AE RMI RMI VF then AD So there is a loop where you can do one or more RMI followed by a VF The loop body is optionally do some RMIs then do a VF the VF after RMI is a re validation So the loop is you can re enter the VF activity after doing some RMIs the process could be modeled as RA VF AE loop RMI VF then after the loop proceed to AD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The process trees loop operator A B means execute A then either exit the loop or execute B and then A again this can happen several times until the loop is exited So A is executed at least once

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: we want to allow zero or more repetitions of the sequence RMI VF after AE That is exactly a loop do RMI VF zero or more times So we can have a loop where the loop body A is the sequence RMI VF and the loop condition B is something In the operator A is executed at least once That doesnt fit because we want zero or more repetitions

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the pattern is a sequence of one or more RMIs then a VF This block can be absent case 2 or present case 1 3 So after AE there is an optional subprocess that is a sequence one or more RMIs then VF

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: How to model one or more RMIs A common pattern RMI RMI gives one or more RMIs execute RMI first then either exit or do RMI again and loop Then the block is sequence one or more RMIs VF And this block is optional so X tau block

Name: Backtracking - NE
Text: Alternatively consider a loop structure A is the main sequence up to AE B is RMI VF That would repeat the entire initial part doesnt fit because after redoing VF we dont go back to RA Another way use a loop where A is main sequence up to AE and B is redo part doesnt match Use a loop around VF and AE the redo path skips AE doesnt fit

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets verify traces Trace 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC take the second branch of XOR the loop RMI RMI gives one RMI then VF then AD SC AC works Trace 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC take tau branch skip block then AD works Trace 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC the loop gives two RMIs then VF works Thus the tree is correct
